caption:
When this dog came to us, he needed more than just a safe place, they needed medical care, patience, and a chance to heal.

We’re already working with our vet to address their medical complications, but these treatments add up quickly. 

He already has over $1000 in expenses for his parvo treatment

Your support helps us say yes to dogs like this, the ones who need a little extra care to survive and thrive.

If you’re able, you can help by giving at fofdogrescue.com. (Link in bio)
Every contribution helps us keep going. 🐾💚

caption:
🚨 We hate having to say this… but we need help.

For a long time, we’ve been operating over capacity. We kept saying yes to dogs in need, even when space and resources were already stretched thin.

Eventually, we reached a point we never wanted to face:

💔 We had to close our doors to new rescues for a while.

Not because the dogs don’t need us.
Not because we don’t want to help.

But because rescue has real costs, and we can only save the dogs we can properly care for.

On average, it costs about $600 to care for one dog during their stay with us to cover food, vet care, vaccines, and daily needs.

🐾 In short, $600 can typically help save another life.

If you’ve ever rooted for one of our dogs, shared a post, or believed in what we do, this is the moment we need you.

💚 Donate if you can.
💚 Share if you can’t.

Every dollar helps us get one step closer to reopening our doors to the next dog who needs a second chance.

Because they’re still out there… waiting.

caption:
Big news 🎉🎉 we just launched our brand new dog rescue…  @fieldsoffreedomrescue !!

Most of you know that we have had a dog rescue here on our farm in central Kentucky for the past 3 years.

We have enjoyed helping to run and experience a large scale rescue  @kentucky.saving.them.together but realize that scaling our operations and impact would be easier (and more fun) if we were able to focus just on the dogs we have here at our farm.

The more dogs we get adopted and into fosters, the more lives we can save.

That being said… along with my friend and co-founder in New York @brittwrobes … and the same awesome team we’ve had at the barn… @kaiuhll @kgrace_515 @dogboy_tim & Eva (who run the day to day ops of caring for the dogs) we announce Fields of Freedom Dog Rescue!

What does this mean?

-Mostly, business as usual… we are still rescuing as many dogs as we can from euthanasia and life threatening situation, our work doesn’t change

-Partnering with Brittany Casella means more dogs can head up to New York and surrounding areas to be adopted. fostered… this opens up our network and efficiencies and allows us to save more dogs here in Kentucky.

-We have new social profiles to follow: Fields of Freedom Dog Rescue on Kentucky & find us here on IG @fieldsoffreedomrescue — we’ll be on Tik Tok etc. soon!

-Fields of freedom is a description of what dogs experience when they first arrive here. For most of them, it’s the first time they’ve felt love or freedom and it’s so beautiful to witness.

-We have a brand new website to check out and learn more: https://www.fofdogrescue.com/

 — you will be seeing more of us online and around the central Kentucky community!!

Our mission stays the same:

We save lives. We educate the community on why a rescue pet can be just as “good” as from a breeder and that all animals deserve respect and common decency. If we change minds, we can start to solve these problems at their root.
alt: Photo shared by Molly Pittman on February 12, 2025 tagging @fieldsoffreedomrescue.

caption:
One year ago this week Jeff and I had our final adventure of this lifetime.

We had a blast of a night, and when it was time to say goodbye, we hugged on Main Street in Danville. The last thing he said was:

	“You know I love you, right?” ♥️♥️

And yes, Dad…I did, do, and have always known. Every single day of my life for 35 years.

How?

Because he didn’t just love he loved unconditionally. He celebrated me every single day, even the parts of me that were different from him… even the parts he didn’t fully agree with. He never tried to change me. He just loved and had a ton of fun while doing it.

This connection we had is the greatest gift of my life.

It gave me the stability and confidence to live a life beyond my wildest dreams. I never thought twice about living in other countries or doing any of the crazy shit I’ve done, because he was always there as my safety net.

Yes, I went out and lived it but he was the foundation underneath it all.

A small town Kentucky girl out in the big world… all because of her dad.

I’m not sure you can give someone a greater gift than he gave me. I’m so grateful. 

In the past year, I’ve learned that the price you pay for a love like this is unfathomable.

I didn’t know pain like this existed.

My heart is still shattered in a million pieces. Some days it feels like there is no oxygen left in the world… like all of the fun that ever existed left when he did…

…and I’m OK with this.

Because it means that you existed. That this was all real and not a movie or a dream.

You spent every day of my life preparing me for the moment you wouldn’t be here, and you succeeded.

I survived.

Whenever I thought about the worst thing that could happen in my life, it was always losing you.

Now I know that I can endure anything.

And if I had to choose, I would do it all over again exactly the same.

We always promised each other that we would find one another in every lifetime and “do it all again.”

That is what keeps me going.

You will always be my greatest love and my greatest loss ♥️♥️♥️ we will continue to spread your love everywhere we go until we see you again.

caption:
386 days.

That’s how long Coco has been here.
386 mornings waking up in rescue.
386 nights falling asleep without a family of her own.

She’s been safe. She’s been loved.
But she’s also been waiting.

Some dogs move through rescue quickly.
Others carry their stories a little longer.
Coco is one of those dogs.

This video isn’t about sadness, it’s about endurance.
About trusting that the right people will come, even when the wait feels endless.

If you’re watching this and something in her eyes feels familiar…
maybe this is the moment her story changes. 🐾💙

caption:
He was abandoned for a month.
Fed by a kind stranger every single day.
Finally caught right before the freezing temperatures took him.
And when no one else could help… we showed up. 💚

Today, Bones is safe, happy, and finally inside, proof that a little love (and a lot of persistence) can change everything. 🐶✨

If this story touched your heart, help us keep doing what we do best: saving dogs who deserve a second chance.

Our holiday wish?
That every single one of our dogs spends this season with a loving family, even a temporary one.

Fostering saves lives.
Fostering heals hearts.
Fostering brings hope.

If you’ve been thinking about fostering… this is your sign. ✨🐾
Let’s make sure no pup spends the holidays alone.

We provide the supplies, you provide the love and affection 💙

DM us if interested!

caption:
Say hello to one of our newest rescues, Pearl.

This big sweetheart was stabbed by a man in a domestic dispute and kicked in the stomach leaving her with severe bruising.

Despite this, she is still as gentle and loving as can be. And can you believe how beautiful her eyes are??

Who's ready to show Pearl what love feels like?
Be intentional about what you’re making and what the objective is. Don’t mix the messages and confuse the audience.

caption:
🚨 URGENT SOS FOR DOG LOVERS 🚨

This is Cain, he just came to us for the third time...

He had to be surrendered because his owner was moving and couldn't keep him. 

Then was returned for having separation anxiety and getting into things when they were gone and they didn't want to try to crate him or give him meds.

And now the adopters' dogs aren't accepting him, so he's stuck in boarding with our vet and you can see how he feels...

This boy is depressed and really needs to find his forever family soon. 

He is a Cane Corso through and through and would make a perfect guard dog and couch potato. 

He is incredibly loyal. Hes grieving and needs to find a new owner to protect and love. 

Please comment below if interested or apply at www.fofdogrescue.com 

Please share as you may know his forever family. 

Cane Corso
3 yr old
Dog friendly w/ females and some males
112 lbs but should be 150 (very underweight)
Older kids (could knock down young kids w his size)
Cat friendly

Please, even if you can't, someone you know might be able to save this sweet boy!

caption:
Dreams do come true… we are now the keepers of an 85 acre farm & forest in the knobs of Central Kentucky! 

The farm is ten minutes from my hometown, a beautiful drive into the countryside to an area my dad @jpitt62 grew up (and my grandmother, great grandmother, and on 😀)… we can trace our family back here a few hundred years ❤️. 

Plans are to start an animal shelter and business/ nature retreat where people can come to relax, reconnect, and have fun. Think rescue animals, treehouses, and cooking around the fire. 

More to come on all of that, but for now just a reminder that dreams do come true!

Creative is the new targeting.
And it’s your biggest lever in 2026.
